title: SyncJobsSchema
description: Background job queue for UI-driven sync. The backend inserts rows here in response to `POST /api/sync/jobs`; the `mixed-up-files worker` child process polls for `pending` rows, executes them (download + ingest + render), and updates state. Every state transition is committed via `CALL DOLT_COMMIT('-Am', 'sync_job: <id> <state>')` so the full history lives in `dolt log` next to the data it produced. See plan-ui-driven-sync.md, Phases D + E.

SyncJobRow.description: One row in the `sync_jobs` table. UI polls `GET /api/sync/jobs/{id}` to render the Lightroom-style progress chrome; cancel flips `state` to `canceled` and the worker SIGTERMs its child.

id.description: Client-or-backend-generated UUIDv4. Used as the row primary key and in `dolt log` commit messages.

source_name.description: `sources[].name` from `config.yaml` this job operates on. NULL for `kind = all` parent jobs and for `kind = ingest` passes that consume every pending download_runs row across sources.

kind.description: What the job does. `download` fetches from a single source's provider; `ingest` translates queued raw files into Dolt rows; `render` re-emits markdown for documents whose cache key changed; `all` is a parent job that enqueues + waits on per-source child jobs.

parent_job_id.description: When this row was enqueued as a child of an `all`-kind job, points at the parent. NULL for standalone jobs. Lets the UI group `Sync all` runs into one collapsible card.

state.description: Lifecycle state. `pending` â†’ `running` when the worker picks it up; `running` â†’ `done` / `failed` at completion; `pending` / `running` â†’ `canceled` when the UI requests a cancel (worker SIGTERMs the child and moves on).

created_at.description: When the backend enqueued the row (ISO-8601 with explicit local offset, per AGENTS.md).

started_at.description: When the worker flipped `state` to `running` (ISO-8601 with explicit offset). NULL while still `pending`.

finished_at.description: When the worker flipped `state` to a terminal value â€” `done`, `failed`, or `canceled` (ISO-8601 with explicit offset). NULL while still pending/running.

error.description: Human-readable error message when `state = failed`. The full structured log lives in `<root>/state/job-logs/<id>.log`; this column is just the summary the UI shows in the chrome.

pid.description: OS pid of the active child process while `state = running`. On worker startup, any `running` rows whose pid is no longer alive get flipped to `failed` (state recovery â€” see plan Phase D, task 16).

progress_pct.description: Latest reported progress, 0.0..1.0. May be NULL when the underlying step can't report a meaningful fraction (e.g. open-ended Slack history pull). Drives the progress bar in the UI chrome.

progress_msg.description: Latest human-readable progress line (e.g. `downloaded 14/200 conversations`). Shown on hover over the chrome's progress bar so the user can see what the worker is actually doing â€” useful for distinguishing 'slow but working' from 'stuck'.
